# Taxiro

> Taxiro is a map-first predictive bike taxi web app MVP for India. It supports ride-now booking, advance bookings, timed ready signals, rider availability, live foreground tracking, private ride-code verification, assigned chat, transparent fare/rider earning splits, UPI/cash collection flow, admin operations, and safety alert foundations.

## Public Pages

- [Home](/): Taxiro landing and app entry.
- [About](/about): Product vision, ride lifecycle, live tracking, MVP limits.
- [Help](/help): Location, booking, payment, safety, and support guidance.
- [Privacy](/privacy): Data collection and privacy notes.
- [Rules](/rules): User/rider rules, misuse rules, cancellation, payment, and safety expectations.

## App Routes

- `/auth`: User/rider sign up and sign in.
- `/dashboard/user`: Authenticated customer booking and active ride workspace.
- `/dashboard/rider`: Authenticated rider workspace.
- `/dashboard/admin`: Role-protected operations dashboard.
- `/rides/[id]`: Authenticated ride details, code, chat, route, payment, and rating context.

## Core Concepts

- Booking modes: ride now and advance booking.
- Ready signals: users publish 15, 30, or 60 minute readiness windows.
- Matching: riders see ready jobs and scheduled demand signals while online.
- Verification: users receive a private 4-digit ride code; riders start the trip only after code verification.
- Tracking: browser foreground GPS and Supabase Realtime power rider-to-pickup and trip-to-drop updates.
- Fare: Rs 7/km standard and Rs 8/km during configured IST peak windows.
- Earnings: Taxiro records 7% company commission and 93% rider earning.
- Safety: SOS, late-trip, and route-change safety-alert foundations notify matching in-app emergency contacts.

## Tech Stack

Next.js App Router, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Supabase Auth/Postgres/PostGIS/RLS/RPC/Realtime, Leaflet, OpenStreetMap, Nominatim, and OSRM.
